checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any fabricated paragraphs or sentences. The text present in sections 1 through 46 is a faithful representation of the source document's content. Section 47 is an AI-generated structural label and error message describing missing pages, which is permitted according to the 'ACCEPTABLE CHANGES' section.

I verified every phone number and URL present in the output against the source PDF. In Section 44 (Appendix A), all 14 phone numbers for Council members and their designees match the source on page 31 exactly. The URLs in Section 5 (page 15), Section 18 (page 22), and Section 28 (page 25) also match the source perfectly. No fabricated contact information was found.

The output is missing the first 10 pages of the source document. The source PDF contains a title page (p. 1), a blank page (p. 2), an Executive Summary (pp. 3-4), a Table of Contents (p. 5), an Introduction (p. 6), Section I (pp. 7-8), Council Membership details (p. 9), and the start of Section II (p. 10). The JSON output begins with the 'Imagery for the State' section from page 11. Section 47 of the output explicitly confirms this omission with the text '[Pages 1-11 unavailable]'. This is a critical failure as it omits significant introductory and summary content.
checks.b_evidence_source: Source PDF pages 1-10 containing the title page, Executive Summary, Table of Contents, Introduction, Section I, and the beginning of Section II.
checks.b_evidence_output: The output starts at page 11 ('Imagery for the State'). Section 47 states: 'Content from pages 1-11 could not be converted. This section may be available after retry.'

checks.a_analysis: The table in Section 44 (Appendix A: Council Members) contains only 10 rows of data. The source document's Appendix A spans pages 31 to 34 and contains a total of 36 data rows (31 members across three pages and 5 advisory members on the fourth). The output has omitted 26 out of 36 rows, which is approximately 72% of the table's data. This exceeds the 20% threshold for a major defect.
checks.b_evidence_source: Appendix A table on pages 31-34 contains 36 rows of data (members and advisory members).
checks.b_evidence_output: The table in Section 44 contains only 10 data rows, ending with 'John Farley'.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Section 44 (Appendix A) is missing a significant amount of text content. As noted in check C4, it only contains data from page 31 of the source, omitting the subsequent pages (32-34) that comprise the remainder of the appendix. The loss of 72% of the content within this section is a major defect.
checks.b_evidence_source: Appendix A spans pages 31-34 of the source document.
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 44 only contains the content from page 31.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text that was successfully converted maintains high fidelity to the source. I found no instances where wording changes altered the meaning of the information provided. Punctuation and whitespace normalization are within the acceptable range of reformatting.

checks.a_analysis: An examination of the document shows no evidence of unintended content duplication. Each section in the JSON corresponds to a specific portion of the source PDF, and no paragraphs or data blocks are repeated beyond their occurrence in the original material.

For the content that was successfully converted, the heading hierarchy remains logical. Major sections use H2 levels, and sub-sections are nested correctly at H3. There are no instances where heading levels are skipped (e.g., jumping from H1 to H3).

The table in 'Appendix A' (listing Council Members) is severely corrupted due to truncation. The source table in the PDF spans four pages (31, 32, 33, and 34). However, the converted output only contains the rows found on page 31, effectively losing three-quarters of the table's structural data without indicating a split or continuation.
checks.b_evidence_source: Table 'Appendix A: Fiscal Year 2013-14 Geographic Information Coordinating Council Members' spanning pages 31-34.
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON table in the section 'Appendix A: Fiscal Year 2013-14 Geographic Information Coordinating Council Members' only contains rows up to 'John Farley' (the end of page 31). Members from pages 32-34 (e.g., Edward Finley, Tony Tata, Sarah Koonts) are missing.

checks.a_analysis: The document structure is fundamentally altered because the entire first quarter of the document is missing from its intended position. Pages 1 through 10, which contain the Title, Executive Summary, Introduction, and the 'Purpose of the Council' section, are omitted from the start of the JSON. Instead, the document erroneously begins with content from page 11. Furthermore, a non-source error message section '[Pages 1-11 unavailable]' is appended to the end of the JSON, creating a highly disjointed and illogical document organization.
checks.b_evidence_source: The document begins with the Title Page (Page 1) followed by the Executive Summary (Page 3).
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON starts with content from Page 11 ('Imagery for the State') and appends an error message '[Pages 1-11 unavailable]' at the end (index 47).

checks.a_analysis: Lists throughout the document, including the metadata committee issues on page 22 and the action plan items on page 26, have been correctly identified and converted into proper list structures, preserving their original type (ordered/unordered) and content.

The URL for the GIS Applications report in the section 'Review of GIS Applications in State Government' is corrupted. The link is split across two separate text objects due to a line break in the source text. While the second object contains the correct 'href', the visible link text is fragmented, which compromises the functional presentation of the document's links.
checks.b_evidence_output: The URL is split into two parts: '...General' and '%20Assembly...'
checks.b_evidence_location: Section 'Review of GIS Applications in State Government', Page 21-22

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the document is severely compromised by the omission of the first 10 pages of body content. The source PDF begins with a cover page, followed by an Executive Summary (page 3), Introduction (page 6), and Section I (page 7). However, the HTML screenshot shows that the body content begins abruptly with the 'Imagery for the State' subsection from Section II (page 11). Although these sections appear in the Table of Contents at the top of the page, the actual content is missing from the body of the rendered HTML. A note at the bottom of the screenshot indicates '[Pages 1-11 unavailable]', confirming this major omission.
checks.b_evidence_source: Executive Summary (p.3), Introduction (p.6), Section I. Purpose of the Geographic Information Coordinating Council (p.7), Section II. Accomplishments (p.10).
checks.b_evidence_output: The body content starts with 'Imagery for the State' and ends with a note '[Pages 1-11 unavailable]'.
checks.b_evidence_location: Start of body content following the Table of Contents

checks.a_analysis: The membership lists in Appendix A and the data update list in Appendix C are rendered correctly in the HTML. The two-column layout for the 'Data and Web Service Updates and New Releases' section (source pages 36-38) maintains the relationship between 'TITLE' and 'CUSTODIAN' and is clear and readable for a sighted user.

Multiple meaningful images present in the source PDF are missing from the HTML output because the pages they reside on were not rendered. This includes the NCGICC logo on the cover page, the 'State Capital and Vicinity' photo (p. 6), Figure 1: A GIS Community of Collaboration (p. 7), Figure 2: Organizational Structure (p. 8), and the photo of 'The Council in a Recent Quarterly Meeting' (p. 9). While images on subsequent pages (Figures 3-7) are present, the absence of these primary diagrams and logos is a major defect in fidelity.
checks.b_evidence_source: NCGICC Logo (p.1), State Capital photo (p.6), Figure 1 (p.7), Figure 2 (p.8), Council Meeting photo (p.9).
checks.b_evidence_output: These images are absent from the HTML rendering.

checks.a_analysis: There is no evidence of body content duplication. The headings appearing in the Table of Contents and again in the body is a standard accessibility feature and does not constitute duplication of content blocks.
